title: The Rise of Big Data
period: 2003–2010s: distributed computing and the data lake
summary: When data outgrew any single machine, a new idea took over: spread it across many cheap computers and store everything raw and cheap. The big data era — and the data lake — was born.

context.headline: Too big for one machine — so use a thousand
context.description: As data exploded in volume, velocity, and variety, the old approach of one bigger machine ran out of road. The breakthrough was distributed computing: spread storage and processing across many cheap machines. Paired with cheap cloud storage, this gave rise to the data lake — store everything raw now, make sense of it later.
context.when: From Google's landmark papers to the cloud data lake
